design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels, defeat enemies to earn evolution points, customize creature body parts (8 upgradeable sections), progress through geological eras with environment-appropriate adaptations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing limited evolution points between immediate survival (healing/damage mitigation) and long-term adaptation (specialization for upcoming challenges). Creatures must evolve to match environmental hazards while competing with crystalline-corrupted dominant species.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Guide a primordial lifeform through 1+ billion years of Earth's evolution, sculpting your own creature design and abilities to overcome environmental obstacles and corrupted evolutionary abominations, ultimately earning partnership with Gaia in paradise
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical fusion of hard evolutionary/geological science framework with mythological creation narrative. The game treats evolution as a moral competition overseen by benevolent deity Gaia, corrupted by external Mars-origin sabotage. No traditional RPG classes—players design creatures organically through combat reward spending. Rejects 'game over'; failure merely deducts progress penalty and respawns via Gaia's intervention.

difficulty.lives_system: No lives system. Character death triggers respawn at map screen with ~50% evolution point penalty deduction
difficulty.continues_system: Infinite continues via Gaia's benevolent respawn mechanic—designed to prevent frustration-based abandonment
difficulty.checkpointing: Map screen acts as checkpoint between levels. Boss encounters at level endpoints require significant preparation
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency. Players customize creature physiology (8 body sections) and choose evolution point allocation independently. Meat consumption provides on-demand healing. Special crystals grant temporary unique forms and evolution point bonuses. Players can grind earlier levels for points before tackling bosses.

progression.upgrade_system: Evolution point allocation system. Defeated enemies drop meat → consume for evolution points → spend on upgrading 8 body sections (head, body, arms, legs, tail, wings, etc.). Upgrades increase stats and unlock new abilities (tail swings, jump height, movement speed). No traditional experience leveling—purely customization-driven.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Geological era progression unlocked sequentially: Paleozoic (fish form) → Mesozoic (dinosaurs) → Neogene (mammals) → Quaternary (humanoids) → Eden (transcendence). Within each era, multiple level chains unlock after boss defeats. Special crystals unlock temporary 'unique body' forms.
replayability_hooks: Book of Life feature: store up to 50 custom creature designs for recall during subsequent playthroughs
Alternative evolution paths within each era based on point allocation strategy
Encounter special crystals that grant temporary powerful forms with unique mechanics
Grind-friendly design allows players to tackle same-era levels multiple times before boss progression
Multiple viable creature specializations (aquatic adaptations vs. terrestrial vs. combat-optimized)

world_building_techniques: Mythological framing: Gaia (Earth personification) guides player through geological history as creation myth
Environmental storytelling: Each era's level design reflects authentic geological periods (Cambrian ocean → Carboniferous forests → Mesozoic deserts → Ice Age tundra → Early human settlements)
Creature design evolution: Visual progression from simple aquatic forms through dinosaurs to mammals to humanoids mirrors scientific evolutionary theory
Antagonistic world-building: Mystery of crystalline corruption reveals external Mars-origin civilization sabotaging Earth's natural evolution—creates meta-narrative conflict
Philosophical dialogue: Gaia's narration explains periods, evolution philosophy, and final revelation that competition for Eden is overseen by benevolent cosmic entity
Final boss characterization: Bolbox (single-celled organism evolved into monstrous humanoid through crystal consumption) embodies corruption of natural order—player victory restores balance

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as period-appropriate evolutionary milestones. Each boss represents an organism transformed into dominance via crystalline corruption—players must overcome both the boss's adapted physiology and demonstrate superior evolutionary fitness. Bosses require more health, damage output, and specialized preparation than standard enemies, forcing tactical evolution point allocation decisions.
notable_bosses: Paleozoic period boss: Crystalline-corrupted aquatic predator
Mesozoic period boss: Corrupted dinosaur (game screenshot shows 'remaining dinosaurs in Ice Age' suggesting chronologically displaced era-ending threat)
Neogene/Ice Age boss: Mammalian corrupted form
Quaternary/Early Man boss: Humanoid-adjacent corrupted creature

Final boss—Bolbox: Gigantic single-celled organism evolved into god-like humanoid form through crystal consumption; reveals itself as orchestrator of all prior corruptions; defeat grants player entry to Eden and Gaia's partnership

faction_indicators: Natural evolutionary order (represented by Gaia): encourages organic creature development, symbiosis with environment, balanced adaptation
Crystalline corruption faction (Mars-origin): represents external domination, artificial power, disruption of natural flow, monstrous transformation
Player character: competes within Gaia's benevolent meritocracy—evolution through combat and adaptation rather than conquest
danger_signals: Crystalline artifacts scattered across levels: visual indicator of external contamination
Corrupted creatures: physically monstrous, disproportionately powerful, unnaturally aggressive
Bolbox's final revelation: single-celled organism achieving god-like status through crystal consumption—represents hubris and denies natural order
Mars civilization mentioned in ending: imply advanced but morally corrupt intelligence interfering with vulnerable worlds
environmental_storytelling: Geological accuracy in level design: each era visually distinct (ocean depths, primordial forests, dinosaur badlands, ice fields, early settlements)
Crystal contamination spreads across map screens: visual accumulation of corruption across geological time
Creature variety reflects actual paleontological diversity: encourages player recognition of authentic species
Paradise realm (Eden) design contrast: transcendent, untainted by crystalline influence, represents ideal evolutionary outcome
